commands.name: docker run
commands.description: Create and start a new container from an image

patterns.text: run a container interactively with a shell
patterns.explanation: Starts an Ubuntu container with an interactive terminal

patterns.text: run a container in the background
patterns.explanation: Starts an nginx container in detached mode

patterns.text: run a container and map a port
patterns.explanation: Maps host port 8080 to container port 80

patterns.text: run a container with a volume mount
patterns.explanation: Mounts a host directory into the container

patterns.text: run a container with environment variables
patterns.explanation: Passes environment variables to the container

patterns.text: run a container that removes itself after exit
patterns.explanation: Automatically removes the container when it stops

commands.name: docker build
commands.description: Build an image from a Dockerfile

patterns.text: build a Docker image from the current directory
patterns.explanation: Builds an image and tags it as myapp:latest

patterns.text: build an image using a specific Dockerfile
patterns.explanation: Uses a custom Dockerfile for the build

patterns.text: build with build arguments
patterns.explanation: Passes build-time variables to the Dockerfile

patterns.text: build without using the cache
patterns.explanation: Forces a fresh build ignoring cached layers

patterns.text: build a multi-platform image
patterns.explanation: Builds images for multiple architectures

commands.name: docker ps
commands.description: List running containers

patterns.text: list all running containers
patterns.explanation: Shows currently running containers

patterns.text: list all containers including stopped ones
patterns.explanation: Shows all containers regardless of their state

patterns.text: show only container IDs
patterns.explanation: Displays only the numeric IDs of running containers

patterns.text: show containers with a custom format
patterns.explanation: Formats output to show only name, status, and ports

patterns.text: filter containers by name
patterns.explanation: Shows only containers whose name matches the filter

commands.name: docker images
commands.description: List images stored locally

patterns.text: list all local Docker images
patterns.explanation: Shows all downloaded and built images

patterns.text: list images with their full digests
patterns.explanation: Includes the image digest hash in the listing

patterns.text: show dangling images with no tag
patterns.explanation: Lists images that are not tagged and not referenced

patterns.text: list images sorted by size
patterns.explanation: Displays images with their sizes sorted from smallest to largest

commands.name: docker pull
commands.description: Download an image from a registry

patterns.text: download the latest version of an image
patterns.explanation: Downloads the nginx:latest image from Docker Hub

patterns.text: pull a specific version of an image
patterns.explanation: Downloads the Node.js 20 Alpine variant

patterns.text: pull an image from a private registry
patterns.explanation: Downloads from a custom container registry

patterns.text: pull all tags for an image
patterns.explanation: Downloads every tagged version of the nginx image

commands.name: docker push
commands.description: Upload an image to a registry

patterns.text: push an image to Docker Hub
patterns.explanation: Uploads the image to your Docker Hub repository

patterns.text: push an image to a private registry
patterns.explanation: Uploads the image to a custom container registry

patterns.text: push all tags of an image
patterns.explanation: Pushes every tagged version of the image

patterns.text: tag and push an image in one workflow
patterns.command: docker tag myapp:latest myuser/myapp:v2.0 && docker push myuser/myapp:v2.0
patterns.explanation: Tags the local image with a new name and pushes it

commands.name: docker exec
commands.description: Execute a command in a running container

patterns.text: open a shell inside a running container
patterns.explanation: Starts an interactive bash shell in the container

patterns.text: run a single command in a container
patterns.explanation: Executes ls inside the container and returns the output

patterns.text: run a command as a specific user in a container
patterns.command: docker exec -u root mycontainer apt-get update
patterns.explanation: Runs the command as root inside the container

patterns.text: set an environment variable for the exec command
patterns.explanation: Passes an environment variable into the executed command

patterns.text: run a detached command in a container
patterns.explanation: Executes the command in the background without attaching

commands.name: docker logs
commands.description: Fetch the logs of a container

patterns.text: view the logs of a container
patterns.explanation: Displays all stdout and stderr output from the container

patterns.text: follow container logs in real time
patterns.explanation: Streams logs as they are produced, like tail -f

patterns.text: show only the last 100 lines of logs
patterns.explanation: Displays the most recent 100 log lines

patterns.text: show logs with timestamps
patterns.explanation: Prepends each log line with a timestamp

patterns.text: view logs since a specific time
patterns.explanation: Shows logs from the last 2 hours

commands.name: docker stop
commands.description: Stop one or more running containers

patterns.text: stop a running container
patterns.explanation: Sends SIGTERM then SIGKILL after a grace period

patterns.text: stop a container with a custom timeout
patterns.explanation: Waits 30 seconds before sending SIGKILL

patterns.text: stop all running containers
patterns.explanation: Stops every currently running container

patterns.text: stop multiple specific containers
patterns.explanation: Stops the named containers simultaneously

commands.name: docker rm
commands.description: Remove one or more containers

patterns.text: remove a stopped container
patterns.explanation: Deletes the container and its writable layer

patterns.text: force remove a running container
patterns.explanation: Kills and removes the container in one step

patterns.text: remove all stopped containers
patterns.explanation: Finds and removes all containers that have exited

patterns.text: remove a container and its volumes
patterns.explanation: Deletes the container along with its anonymous volumes

commands.name: docker rmi
commands.description: Remove one or more images

patterns.text: delete a local Docker image
patterns.explanation: Removes the specified image from local storage

patterns.text: force remove an image even if containers use it
patterns.explanation: Forcefully removes the image regardless of references

patterns.text: remove all dangling images
patterns.explanation: Deletes all untagged and unreferenced images

patterns.text: remove all images
patterns.explanation: Removes every local image

commands.name: docker compose
commands.description: Define and run multi-container applications with Docker Compose

patterns.text: start all services defined in docker-compose.yml
patterns.explanation: Builds, creates, and starts all services in detached mode

patterns.text: stop and remove all compose services
patterns.explanation: Stops containers and removes containers, networks, and default volumes

patterns.text: rebuild and restart services
patterns.explanation: Rebuilds images before starting the services

patterns.text: view logs from all compose services
patterns.explanation: Follows the combined log output of all services

patterns.text: scale a service to multiple instances
patterns.explanation: Runs 3 instances of the web service

patterns.text: run a one-off command in a service container
patterns.explanation: Opens a shell in the running web service container

commands.name: docker network
commands.description: Manage Docker networks

patterns.text: list all Docker networks
patterns.explanation: Shows all networks including bridge, host, and custom ones

patterns.text: create a custom bridge network
patterns.explanation: Creates a user-defined bridge network for container communication

patterns.text: connect a running container to a network
patterns.explanation: Attaches the container to an additional network

patterns.text: inspect a network to see connected containers
patterns.explanation: Shows detailed config and which containers are attached

patterns.text: remove unused networks
patterns.explanation: Deletes all networks not used by any container

commands.name: docker volume
commands.description: Manage Docker volumes for persistent storage

patterns.text: list all Docker volumes
patterns.explanation: Shows all volumes on the system

patterns.text: create a named volume
patterns.explanation: Creates a persistent volume named mydata

patterns.text: inspect a volume to see its mount point
patterns.explanation: Shows the volume details including the host path

patterns.text: remove unused volumes to free disk space
patterns.explanation: Deletes all volumes not currently used by a container

patterns.text: remove a specific volume
patterns.explanation: Deletes the named volume and its data

commands.name: docker inspect
commands.description: Return low-level information on Docker objects

patterns.text: see full details of a container
patterns.explanation: Returns complete JSON metadata about the container

patterns.text: get the IP address of a container
patterns.explanation: Extracts the container IP using a Go template

patterns.text: inspect an image for its layers and config
patterns.explanation: Shows image metadata including layers, env vars, and entry point

patterns.text: get the mount points of a container
patterns.explanation: Shows all volume and bind mounts for the container

commands.name: docker system
commands.description: Manage Docker system resources

patterns.text: see how much disk space Docker is using
patterns.explanation: Shows disk usage by images, containers, volumes, and build cache

patterns.text: clean up all unused Docker resources
patterns.explanation: Removes all unused images, containers, networks, and build cache

patterns.text: prune with volume cleanup too
patterns.explanation: Removes everything unused including volumes

patterns.text: show real-time resource usage of containers
patterns.explanation: Displays a live stream of CPU, memory, and network stats

patterns.text: show Docker system information
patterns.explanation: Displays system-wide configuration and resource details

commands.name: kubectl get
commands.description: Display one or many Kubernetes resources

patterns.text: list all pods in the current namespace
patterns.explanation: Shows all pods with their status, restarts, and age

patterns.text: list pods across all namespaces
patterns.explanation: Shows pods from every namespace

patterns.text: list all services
patterns.explanation: Displays services with their types, cluster IPs, and ports

patterns.text: get resources in YAML format
patterns.explanation: Outputs the full YAML definition of the deployment

patterns.text: watch pods for real-time changes
patterns.explanation: Continuously watches for pod status changes

commands.name: kubectl apply
commands.description: Apply a configuration to a resource by file or stdin

patterns.text: apply a Kubernetes manifest file
patterns.explanation: Creates or updates resources defined in the YAML file

patterns.text: apply all manifests in a directory
patterns.explanation: Applies every YAML file in the directory

patterns.text: apply a manifest from a URL
patterns.explanation: Downloads and applies the remote manifest

patterns.text: apply with a dry run to preview changes
patterns.explanation: Validates the manifest without actually applying it

patterns.text: apply a kustomization
patterns.explanation: Applies a Kustomize configuration directory

commands.name: kubectl logs
commands.description: Print the logs for a container in a pod

patterns.text: view logs of a specific pod
patterns.explanation: Displays the stdout/stderr output of the pod's container

patterns.text: follow pod logs in real time
patterns.explanation: Streams logs as they are produced

patterns.text: view logs from a specific container in a multi-container pod
patterns.explanation: Shows logs from the sidecar container

patterns.text: view logs from a previous crashed container
patterns.explanation: Shows logs from the last terminated instance of the container

patterns.text: get logs from all pods matching a label
patterns.explanation: Aggregates logs from all pods with the given label

commands.name: kubectl exec
commands.description: Execute a command in a container inside a pod

patterns.text: open a shell inside a Kubernetes pod
patterns.explanation: Starts an interactive bash session in the pod

patterns.text: run a single command inside a pod
patterns.explanation: Executes the command and prints the output

patterns.text: exec into a specific container in a multi-container pod
patterns.explanation: Opens a shell in the sidecar container specifically

patterns.text: run a command in a pod in a specific namespace
patterns.explanation: Executes in a pod within the production namespace

commands.name: kubectl describe
commands.description: Show details of a specific resource or group of resources

patterns.text: get detailed information about a pod
patterns.explanation: Shows events, conditions, volumes, and container details

patterns.text: describe a node to check resource usage
patterns.explanation: Shows capacity, allocatable resources, and running pods

patterns.text: describe a service to check endpoints
patterns.explanation: Shows service type, selector, ports, and backend endpoints

patterns.text: describe all pods matching a label
patterns.explanation: Shows detailed info for every pod with that label

commands.name: kubectl port-forward
commands.description: Forward one or more local ports to a pod

patterns.text: forward a local port to a pod
patterns.explanation: Makes the pod's port 80 accessible on localhost:8080

patterns.text: forward a port to a service
patterns.explanation: Forwards traffic to the service on localhost:3000

patterns.text: forward to a pod in a specific namespace
patterns.explanation: Forwards a database port from a production namespace pod

patterns.text: forward and listen on all interfaces
patterns.explanation: Listens on all network interfaces, not just localhost
